verdict.answer_first: A leads with the usable bottom line: "CAP only forces a choice during a network partition... real choice is C vs. A"; B starts clearly too, but less directly actionable.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits harder: "decide CP vs. AP per category of data... correct answer"; B adds a multi-step list plus a follow-up CTA.
verdict.fabrication: B invents an unsupported number: "99%+ of the time"; A has no concrete unverifiable number of that kind.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to save as a prescription: "Does this data harm someone... lean CP" / "staying up more important... lean AP" plus the final per-category rule.
